title: hieracium suecicum subsp. bergense
intro: Hieracium suecicum subsp. bergense, commonly known as the hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Hieracium suecicum subsp. bergense can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues.
